source: Curated for Constellation. Chemistry sourced from Cartographer's neutral_losses dict (cartographer/masses.py); ion-type-only and K-Ub remnant losses excluded — those route through the IonType enum or LossRegistry.register_custom respectively.

neutral_losses.name: Water loss
neutral_losses.description: Hydroxyl side-chain water loss — common from S/T/D/E containing fragments. Standard 18.011 Da neutral loss.

neutral_losses.name: Ammonia loss
neutral_losses.description: Amine side-chain ammonia loss — common from K/R/N/Q containing fragments. Standard 17.027 Da neutral loss.

neutral_losses.name: Metaphosphate loss
neutral_losses.description: Metaphosphate (HPO3, ~79.97 Da) loss from any phosphorylated residue (S/T/Y). Licensed by the presence of UNIMOD:21 (Phospho) anywhere in the fragment.

neutral_losses.name: Phosphoric acid loss
neutral_losses.description: Phosphoric acid (H3PO4, ~97.98 Da) loss via β-elimination from phospho-S or phospho-T. Requires the phospho mod to sit on an S or T residue inside the fragment (Y phospho cannot β-eliminate).
